Transcrição Completa: Keep Your Moral High

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "24 Keep Your Moral High"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título com ícones de motivação e moral alta

[00:02] Okay, so in sales, one of the most important things that you could possibly do is keeping your morale high.

[00:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "The Hydra Strategy Challenge"

NÚMEROS: Hundreds of leads per week

VISUAL: Fat cat closers vs real salespeople, volume de leads aumentando

[00:15] So when we first come into businesses, especially businesses that have fat cat closers, the biggest issue is we'll introduce strategies like the Hydra, which is where a fuckload of people will fill out a lead form every day.

[00:28] They'll answer a handful of very direct questions interested in what the offer is. And the salespeople instead of just getting a bunch of scheduled calls in their calendar from people who watched videos, they'll have hundreds of people that they need to communicate with within a week.

[00:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Real Salesperson Activities"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Lista de atividades: scheduling calls, texting, following up

[00:45] And they'll need to get calls scheduled. They'll need to have sales calls sporadically. They'll need to text people back and forth. They'll need to be following up. They'll need to do what a real salesperson will do.

[00:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Morale Drop = Performance Cancer"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Moral baixo espalhando como câncer pela equipe

[01:00] And what I've seen, and it's a true like rapid spreading cancer, is their morale drops. When the morale drops, there is no possibility a sales team will perform well. Absolutely no possibility of that.

[01:15] If the leaders of the organization are in a desperate position or if the salespeople themselves are in a desperate position where they have to get results, they have to get money today, that morale is just going to keep getting knocked down and knocked down.

[01:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Leadership's Essential Responsibility"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: CEO/líder mantendo moral alta da equipe

[01:35] Sales do not happen when sales people have low morale. The CEO of the organization or whoever's leading the sales team, their essential responsibility outside of just training and making their people great is to keep morale high.

[01:50] You have to constantly be acknowledging the momentum you've created. You have to constantly be acknowledging all of the positive actions, the positive results that you have done.

[02:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "NBA Games = Morale Laboratory"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Miami Heat games, courtside experience

[02:05] I go to NBA games quite frequently. I live here in Miami. I go to the Miami Heat games all the time. And we typically sit on the floor right by the player benches. And it's so interesting to watch in real time when you look at NBA games, it's really a game of who has the higher morale.

[02:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Heat vs Celtics Example"

NÚMEROS: Down 9-10 points most of game

VISUAL: Exemplo específico Heat vs Celtics, comeback no 4º quarto

[02:30] So just recently, last year the Boston Celtics beat the Miami Heat in the semifinals. This season here in 2022, the Heat and the Boston Celtics just played one another for the second regular season game.

[02:45] The Heat were down by about 9 to 10 points throughout essentially the entire game. During the fourth quarter though, the Heat, they really pick up. In the last two to five minutes of the fourth quarter, almost every game, the Heat just start going crazy.

[03:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Momentum Shift in Real Time"

NÚMEROS: 2-3 shots in a row, 2-3 defensive stops

VISUAL: Energia do estádio mudando, jogadores correndo mais rápido

[03:10] As an example, if the Heat are doing really well, they'll make two to three shots in a row, they'll come back down on defense, they'll prevent the Boston Celtics from scoring for maybe two to three runs in a row, and then they'll go back, maybe they'll make a three.

[03:25] And everybody just starts losing their shit. It's like I get chills right now even just thinking about the level of energy that raises in the entire stadium. You see it in the players. You watch them run faster. Everybody's more excited. People are standing in their seats.

[03:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "The Opposite Effect"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Estádio esvaziando de energia, jogadores mais desajeitados

[03:50] Everything changes versus in just a few plays. If the Heat miss a few shots, if the Celtics make a few threes, or god forbid that you just get dunked on, the opposite happens. The entire stadium feels drained, just like the life gets sucked out of people.

[04:10] Everybody sits down. You can hear people saying things like, "Man, that was a terrible shot. How are these people even in the game right now?" You can see it in the players. They seem almost more clumsy when they don't have high morale.

[04:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Morale Shifts Happen Fast"

NÚMEROS: 2-3 plays can change everything

VISUAL: Mudanças rápidas de moral em poucas jogadas

[04:30] You're watching the other team in a stadium full of fans who hate them start to thrive. You get to watch the Boston Celtics just take off. They create their own energy and they keep their own morale high. But it can happen in a few plays.

[04:45] So to be clear, it's very important that you acknowledge when salespeople, they can die out fast. Just as in basketball, we can watch in real time when a team in just simply like two to three plays in a row can go from a huge shift in morale upward to a huge shift in morale downward.

[05:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Sales Reality Check"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Dias ruins: sem vendas, no-shows, apanhar mentalmente

[05:10] We in sales have to make sure that we are constantly on the up and up. There will be days where you don't make sales. There will be days where people don't show up for calls. There will be days where you just kind of feel like you got the shit kicked out of you.

[05:25] You better wake up that next day and have some actions that build the morale of your organization and of you as a salesperson. Because if you don't, it's a high probability you're going to do terrible at your job, waste a fuckload of ad spend for the business.

[05:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Environmental Morale Boosters"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Exemplos do Heat: música, chants "defense", noise meter

[05:45] And it's not because you're bad at what you do. It's because your morale is low. We have to keep morale high. Back to the Miami Heat. When people in the stadium who work there can recognize the morale is low, people step in and they make changes to the environment.

[06:00] So, as an example, the people who run the sound clips, they'll start playing chants like "defense, defense." They'll try to get everybody to hype up again. They'll play certain songs when there's timeouts that have a little more energy.

[06:15] They'll put that thing up on the big screen and it says noise level and when people see it, their goal is to try to be as loud as they can to get the noise thing to go as high as they can.

[06:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Jeremy's Personal System"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Sistema de som, Thunder Struck, Lil Baby

[06:32] So like right here in this very room, I have a crazy sound system. I'll come in here if my personal morale is low for whatever reason, I'll fucking crank it up. I'll play a song like Thunder Struck or I'll throw some Lil Baby on there.

[06:45] And it's hard to be in an environment where there's something like hype music and to just be sitting there with your morale all low. It's easy to have low morale when everybody around you has low morale.

[07:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Environment vs Individual Responsibility"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Ambiente influenciando moral vs responsabilidade individual

[07:05] It's hard to maintain low morale when there's a bunch of people around you in the environment around you pushing your morale higher and keeping you in the fucking zone. That's where you have to be.

[07:18] If you recognize your morale is low, you better get that morale up. You better help the people around you keep their morale up because they will affect you. Your environment will affect you.

[07:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Number One Prerogative"

NÚMEROS: #1 priority

VISUAL: Prioridade máxima: manter moral alto

[07:35] You, more importantly than anybody else will affect you. And if your morale is low, sales will not occur. You will be absolutely terrible at your job. Your number one prerogative in the day besides make money and close deals, keep the fucking morale high. Makes a huge difference.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy revela a VERDADE BRUTAL que vendedores ignoram - moral baixo = MORTE CERTA nas vendas! A analogia do Heat vs Celtics é DEVASTADORA - mostra como 2-3 jogadas podem mudar TUDO. Jeremy entende que vendas são 80% psicologia, 20% técnica. O sistema de som no escritório é GENIAL - ambiente controlando estado mental. Jeremy não aceita desculpas - você É responsável pelo seu próprio moral. Pura DOMINÂNCIA mental!
